content: Drug price comparisons reveal stark differences: insulin costs ~$300/vial in US vs $30-80 in Canada/Europe; cancer immunotherapies $150,000-200,000/year in US vs 30-50% lower elsewhere. US pharmaceutical spending per capita ($1,400) is 2-3x higher than other high-income countries despite similar utilization. Factors driving US prices: patent evergreening (minor modifications extending exclusivity), delayed generic entry (pay-for-delay settlements), lack of price negotiation historically, and PBM rebate structures incentivizing high list prices. The WHO estimates 1.3 billion people globally cannot access essential medicines due to cost, with cancer drugs particularly unaffordable even in high-income countries.
